name: Priya Mehta
investment_thesis: We back technical pre-seed founders building developer infrastructure and AI-native tooling. Our ideal investment is a founding team of 2-3 engineers who have identified a painful workflow problem inside a large engineering organization and are building the tool they wish had existed. We prioritize proprietary technical approaches, open-source community traction, and clear evidence of founder-market fit. We do not invest in consumer products.

founder_profiles: solo non-technical founder
no relevant domain experience
no technical co-founder at pre-seed

name: Marcus Chen
investment_thesis: Seed-stage fintech and payments infrastructure. We believe financial infrastructure is being rebuilt from scratch over the next decade. We back founders who understand payment rails, credit risk, or regulatory mechanics deeply and have credible distribution advantages — either through an existing customer network, a novel go-to-market, or a licensed entity. B2B fintech is our sweet spot. Consumer fintech only if the CAC model is fundamentally different from incumbents.

founder_profiles: no financial services experience
no prior company building experience
first-time founder without fintech domain knowledge

name: Dr. Rachel Torres
investment_thesis: We invest at the intersection of clinical workflows and software at seed and Series A. Healthcare companies that never touch the clinical workflow won't earn durable contracts. We prioritize startups where the founder has lived the problem — clinician-entrepreneurs, health system operators, or deep payer/benefits experts. Digital health and health IT with evidence of clinical outcomes are our primary focus.

founder_profiles: no clinical or healthcare operations background
no pathway to regulatory clearance
consumer wellness without clinical evidence

name: James Osei
investment_thesis: Pre-seed through Series A climate tech. We back founders building the infrastructure layer of the energy transition: grid software, carbon accounting, green finance, supply chain decarbonization, and industrial process electrification. The best climate companies will be built by people who have operated inside the industries they're disrupting — utilities, heavy industry, logistics, agriculture. Pure software plays in climate are increasingly interesting when they enable policy-driven mandates (Scope 3, CSRD, SEC climate rules).

founder_profiles: no domain experience in energy, industrials, or climate policy

name: Sofia Reyes
investment_thesis: Seed-stage consumer and marketplace companies in the US. We back founders who understand a specific consumer behavior shift and can demonstrate organic growth before or just after launch. Strong preference for founders who are the user: personal origin story, community-native go-to-market, and network effects baked into the acquisition strategy. We don't invest in consumer fintech, food delivery, or subscription boxes.

founder_profiles: enterprise sales background as only prior experience
no consumer product or community background
subscription box or food delivery

name: David Park
investment_thesis: Seed and Series A enterprise software in security, compliance, and workflow automation. We back B2B SaaS companies targeting the mid-market and enterprise with a clear champion in IT, Legal, or Security. Strong preference for companies with a regulated industry angle — financial services, healthcare, defense, critical infrastructure — where compliance creates a durable switching cost. AI-native tools replacing legacy enterprise software are a primary thesis area.

founder_profiles: no prior enterprise sales exposure
solo non-technical founder building security infrastructure

name: Nadia Petrov
investment_thesis: We lead seed and Series A rounds in B2B and consumer marketplace companies where liquidity dynamics are genuinely defensible. Our bar is evidence of a liquidity flywheel: either the supply is scarce and hard to replicate, or the demand aggregation creates a new distribution advantage. Both sides of the marketplace need a clear reason to stay rather than transact off-platform.

founder_profiles: no prior marketplace or two-sided platform experience
marketplace without clear liquidity strategy

name: Aaron Nguyen
investment_thesis: Seed and early Series A in cybersecurity only. We only do cybersecurity — full stop. Our LPs are ex-CISOs and our deal sourcing runs through that network. We have particular conviction in supply chain security, OT/ICS security, identity, and cloud-native detection and response — areas where legacy vendors are under-investing or where regulatory tailwinds are accelerating adoption.

founder_profiles: no prior security industry experience
no technical cybersecurity background
security as a feature of another product

name: Christine Wallace
investment_thesis: Generalist angel with 18 years of operating experience across SaaS, marketplaces, and consumer. I co-invest alongside institutional leads at pre-seed and seed. I look for founders who have done something hard before and have an unfair distribution or insight advantage. Not a thesis investor — I back people, not sectors. I write small checks and I add value through intros and candid feedback.

founder_profiles: no evidence of prior execution in any domain
idea-stage with no co-founder and no technical ability
pivoted three or more times with no learning articulated

name: Ben Whitfield
investment_thesis: Series A in SaaS, AI infrastructure, and fintech. We invest when a company has demonstrated product-market fit and is ready to build a repeatable go-to-market. Our bar at Series A: $1-3M ARR, strong NRR (above 120%), evidence of at least one repeatable ICP, and a founding team that has thought seriously about org design. We follow on through Series C. Not interested in consumer. Not interested in hardware.

founder_profiles: pre-revenue or very early traction with no ARR signal
no go-to-market discipline or ICP definition

name: Keiko Fujimoto
investment_thesis: We bridge US early-stage companies (seed and Series A) to the Japanese and Southeast Asian enterprise market. Our value-add is proprietary: we have signed partnership agreements with 12 Japanese corporations — including 3 trading houses and 2 major banks — that act as customer-investors for portfolio companies entering Japan. We prefer enterprise B2B SaaS, fintech, and deep tech where US product leadership translates to a Japan market advantage. We co-invest alongside US leads.

founder_profiles: no interest in international expansion
US-only market thesis with no stated interest in Asia
consumer-only go-to-market

name: Thomas Bergmann
investment_thesis: Seed through Series A in data infrastructure, logistics tech, and industrial software. We look for companies winning in the unsexy middle of the economy — logistics, manufacturing, supply chain, energy — where software penetration is still low and switching costs are high once you're embedded. We don't need category-defining markets; we need durable, high-margin contracts and a path to $50M+ ARR from a specific industry vertical.

founder_profiles: no operations or industry domain experience in target vertical
academic background without commercial deployment experience

name: Patricia Kim
investment_thesis: Pre-seed and seed in B2B SaaS and developer tools. I only back repeat founders or domain experts with a clear unfair advantage. Strong preference for technical co-founders. I will not back a pre-revenue consumer app. The market is crowded, the CAC is high, and I have seen the unit economics on subscription consumer products more times than I care to count.

founder_profiles: first-time founders without a technical co-founder
pre-revenue B2C consumer company
no domain expertise in their stated market
vague or generic product description

name: Lars Eriksen
investment_thesis: Seed and Series A in robotics, hardware-enabled software, and deep tech. We back founders who are building physical-world automation and are comfortable with the capital intensity and timeline of hardware development. We look for a clear path to gross margins above 50% via a software or Robots-as-a-Service layer on top of the hardware. Strong preference for applications in manufacturing, logistics, agriculture, and defense-adjacent areas.

founder_profiles: no hardware or engineering background
no prior deep tech or physical-world experience
pure software founder claiming to build robotics

name: Alex Rivera
investment_thesis: Web3-native infrastructure and protocol-level investments. We do not invest in consumer applications, meme coins, or web2 SaaS companies that have added a token. We back teams building the base layer of the decentralized stack: L2s, ZK proofs, DeFi infrastructure, cross-chain interoperability, and onchain developer tooling.

founder_profiles: web2 background with no crypto-native experience or community
token as an afterthought on a SaaS product

name: Sandra Okafor
investment_thesis: Growth equity at Series B and beyond in SaaS, AI infrastructure, and fintech. We invest when companies have $5M+ ARR, strong NRR, and are ready to build international distribution. Our value-add is go-to-market: we have operating partners who have built GTM teams in EMEA and APJ, and an LP base that includes large corporates who can become customers or distribution partners. We do not do early stage. We do not do consumer.

founder_profiles: pre-revenue or seed stage
no international expansion readiness or strategy
under $3M ARR

name: Robert Kozlowski
investment_thesis: We back Midwest-founded enterprise software companies at seed and Series A. The Midwest is chronically underfunded and over-indexed toward industrial, logistics, healthcare, and agricultural enterprises — exactly the sectors where software penetration is lowest. We believe the next generation of durable enterprise SaaS businesses will be built in Detroit, Columbus, Chicago, and Minneapolis, not in San Francisco. Preference for companies whose customers operate in the US Midwest.

founder_profiles: no connection to Midwest market or customers
consumer or B2C focus with no enterprise angle
coastal founder with no stated interest in Midwest customers
